> Patch Set 5:
> 
> Failed enforced test custom-101 on RHEL 8.7/x86_64 uploaded by Trevis Autotest 2 from trevis-23vm2: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/07fb193e-8249-4ec6-9ede-f7aadf706ded ran 3 tests.  1 tests failed: sanity-krb5.

The failure in sanity-krb5 test_151 is due to a missing Kerberos principal. In this test, the MGS is being kerberized: it gets configured to only accept connections from servers and clients if they use the correct Kerberos flavor. But this can work only if the MGS server has its own principal, in the form:

   lustre_mgs/<node>@<DOMAIN>

So for sanity-krb5 test_151 to pass in Maloo, the MGS specific principal must be declared in the KDC, and exported to keytab files, just like with other principals for MDS, OSS and client roles.

parents.subject: LU-9859 libcfs: move percpt_lock into lnet

commit.subject: LU-16799 tests: fix sanity-krb5
commit.message:
LU-16799 tests: fix sanity-krb5

sanity-krb5.sh needs to be fixed in several ways.
It cannot assume that the Kerberos credentials cache is FILE, and
expect ccache files to be under /tmp/krb5cc_*.
The lsvcgssd daemon must be launched with -vvv flags for easier
debugging.
Keyring needs to be cleared appropriately after using 'lfs flushctx'.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-krb5 kerberos=true
Signed-off-by: Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>
Change-Id: I31ca8d2d97e137c7ba9fa478d5432aeedb5135a8
